ai_copilot_chat.title: AI Design Copilot Chat
ai_copilot_chat.description: Natural language CAD commands. Convert English descriptions into 3D geometry instantly.

examples.output: Spur gear with 24 teeth, module 2, 10mm bore

ai_copilot_chat.usage:
1. Press Ctrl+K to open copilot
2. Type your command
3. Press Enter to create geometry
4. Geometry appears in viewport instantly
tips: Typos are OK: 'geer', 'dieameter', 'sylindr' all work
Units are always in mm
Use formulas in parameters: 'gear with bore =wall_thickness*5'
Undo with Ctrl+Z if you don't like the result

physics_simulation.title: Physics Simulation
physics_simulation.description: Real-time drop testing with stress analysis. Identify where parts will fail under impact.
physics_simulation.usage:
1. Press Ctrl+P to start simulation
2. Objects fall due to gravity
3. Watch for color changes on impact
4. Red areas indicate failure points
5. Optimize design to reduce red stress
tips: Physics runs in background, doesn't block modeling
Bounce coefficient is 0.6 (60% energy rebound)
Each impact accumulates stress on meshes
Combine with Parameter Table to iterate quickly
Export stressmap as texture for analysis

generative_design.title: Generative Design
generative_design.description: Auto-generate optimized topology with constraints. Create organic lattice structures that minimize material while maintaining stiffness.

material_budget.meaning: Percentage of original volume to retain

iterations.meaning: Number of optimization cycles (higher = better but slower)

generative_design.usage:
1. Press Ctrl+G
2. Set material budget (e.g., 50%)
3. Set iterations (e.g., 30)
4. Click 'Generate Optimized Structure'
5. Watch progress bar as algorithm runs
6. Result is a strut-based lattice
tips: More iterations = better results but takes longer
Lower budget = sparser, more artistic result
Export lattice as STL for 3D printing
Can layer multiple generative steps for refinement
Combine with Physics to validate optimized design

cost_estimator.title: Real-time Cost Estimator
cost_estimator.description: Live manufacturing cost calculation for three methods: CNC, 3D printing, and injection molding.
cost_estimator.usage:
1. Create or edit a part
2. Cost estimator updates automatically
3. See three prices in real-time
4. Bottom shows recommended method
5. Modify geometry and watch costs change
tips: Always visible at top-right corner
Updates every 1 second as you model
Use for ROI analysis: 'At what volume does tooling pay off?'
Consider post-processing when estimating total cost
Material changes: titanium is ~5× steel cost

smart_snap.title: Smart Snap & Auto-Dimension
smart_snap.description: Intelligent snapping recognizes design intent. Auto-detects bolt circles and linear arrays. Auto-places dimensions on drawings.
smart_snap.usage:
1. Drag part towards another part
2. Within 15px, snap preview appears
3. Release to snap into place
4. Bolt circles auto-detect during drawing generation
tips: Snap distance is configurable: 15px default
Pattern detection is automatic, no manual input needed
Drawings auto-dimension detected patterns
Use for rapid assembly mockups
Combine with smart mate for full assembly workflow

version_control.title: Version Control Visual Diff
version_control.description: Git-like CAD branching with visual geometry comparison. Track design iterations and rollback changes.
version_control.usage:
1. Click 'Save Version' button
2. Version appears in history with timestamp
3. Switch branches from dropdown
4. Click 'Show Diff' to compare last 2 versions
5. Geometry changes highlighted with colors
6. Click any version to restore
tips: Save after every major milestone
Use descriptive branch names: feature/weight-opt, bugfix/hole-offset
Visual diff is instant, no git learning curve
Export version history as JSON for archival
Branching is non-destructive: always have a fallback

parametric_table.title: Parametric Table
parametric_table.description: Excel-like parameter editor with formula support. Create families of parts with one spreadsheet.
parametric_table.usage:
1. Press Ctrl+T to open table
2. Edit values directly in cells
3. Add formulas with = prefix
4. Press Enter to recalculate
5. Geometry updates live in viewport
6. Export/Import CSV for sharing
tips: Formulas are live: change width, height auto-updates
CSV roundtrip: Export → Excel → Import
Create family of sizes: S, M, L variants
Use for design exploration: test 100 variations in 5 minutes
Parameters feed into BOM and cost estimates

smart_assembly_mating.title: Smart Assembly Mating
smart_assembly_mating.description: Drag-to-snap assembly with auto-detected mate types. No manual positioning needed.
smart_assembly_mating.usage:
1. Click and drag part A towards part B
2. System analyzes geometry
3. Within 20px, snap preview shows
4. System auto-selects mate type
5. Release to snap and apply mate
tips: Mate type is automatic based on geometry analysis
Snap distance is 20px (quite forgiving)
Visual preview before snapping prevents mistakes
Use for rapid assembly mockups
Undo with Ctrl+Z if snap was wrong

manufacturing_drawings.title: Manufacturing Drawings Auto-Generator
manufacturing_drawings.description: One-click ISO/ANSI engineering drawings with all technical details. Send directly to machine shop.
manufacturing_drawings.usage:
1. Complete your design in 3D
2. Menu: File → Generate Drawing
3. Wait 2 seconds for rendering
4. Drawing appears in new window
5. Click 'Download' to save PNG
6. Send to machine shop or 3D print service
tips: One-click: no configuration needed
Auto-dimensions: critical features highlighted
ISO standard: recognized worldwide
Export as PNG: edit in Photoshop if needed
BOM is live: changes in model → BOM auto-updates

digital_twin_live_data.title: Digital Twin Live Data
digital_twin_live_data.description: Real-time IoT sensor visualization on 3D model. Monitor temperature, vibration, and wear in real-time.
digital_twin_live_data.usage:
1. Enable: Menu View → Digital Twin
2. HUD appears showing live data
3. Model colors reflect temperature
4. Watch vibration scale animation
5. Track wear percentage over time
tips: Simulation mode: test with fake data (default)
Real sensors: connect via WebSocket
Color feedback is instant visual status
Export data: CSV for predictive analysis
Predictive maintenance: use wear trends to schedule service
Multi-sensor: monitor 4+ bearings simultaneously

quick_start.tutorial_1_minute: Press Ctrl+K, type 'gear 24 teeth', press Enter
quick_start.tutorial_5_minutes: Create a part → Press Ctrl+P → Watch physics → Check costs
quick_start.tutorial_10_minutes: Full workflow: Copilot → Physics → Generative → Costs → Export Drawing

troubleshooting.solution: Use simpler language: 'gear 24 teeth' instead of 'create a gearwheel with 24 teeth and a 5mm bore'
troubleshooting.details: The parser looks for specific keywords (gear, bracket, cylinder) and numerical values

troubleshooting.solution: Reduce number of meshes or disable for a moment
troubleshooting.details: Physics runs on all objects. Hide non-essential parts to improve speed

troubleshooting.solution: Check that snap is enabled and parts are within 15 pixels
troubleshooting.details: Move your parts closer together. Snap distance is 15px by default

troubleshooting.solution: Ensure your part has clear geometry and no internal holes
troubleshooting.details: Auto-dimension works best on simple shapes. Complex cavities may not be detected
Formulas are JavaScript expressions. Avoid spaces around operators

tips_and_tricks: Combine AI Copilot + Generative Design to create optimized parts in 1 minute
Use Parameter Table to create a family of 10 sizes from one design
Physics + Parameter Table = rapid iteration on robust designs
Version Control branching: feature/weight-opt, feature/faster-assembly, etc.
Manufacturing Drawings export to PDF: File → Generate Drawing → Save → Print
Smart Snap works best with similar-sized parts; adjust snap distance if needed
Digital Twin: export live data to CSV, use Excel for trend analysis
Use Cost Estimator to decide: 1 part = 3D Print, 1000 parts = Injection Mold
